items.title: NST duo join 2026 Khazanah Wolfson programme at Cambridge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: New Straits Times specialist writer Iylia Marsya Iskandar and Business Times editor Muhammed Ahmad Hamdan have been selected for the 2026 Khazanah Wolfson Press Fellowship. Khazanah managing director Datuk Amirul Feisal Wan Zahir said journalists today play a critical role in navigating a complex information landscape. She said the selection of two NST journalists was recognition of both individual talent and newsroom standards.

items.title: Another four years before flood situation in Selangor improves, says exco
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Long-term flood mitigation efforts are still underway in Selangor and it will take at least four years before the results can be seen, the state government said. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the state acknowledged concerns raised by Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, over the persistent flood issues, but added that works are ongoing to address the matter. "Flood problems in Selangor are unlikely to be fully resolved anytime soon, as major mitigation projects remain ongoing," Izham told reporters after the state legislative assembly meeting here today (Tuesday).

items.title: Police seize RM4.6mil worth of vape devices and drugs in Op Vape crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have seized 8,091 vape devices, 5,257 cartridges and 19.67kg of drugs, including 18.55kg of liquid believed to contain furanyl fentanyl, in a nationwide crackdown under Op Vape 1.0 conducted from April 13 to 17. Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said 20 people were arrested for various drug-related offences following inspections of 1,670 vape premises nationwide. "Of those detained, 13 were arrested under Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act, five under Section 39B for drug trafficking, one under Section 12(2), and another under the Poisons Act," he told reporters here on Tuesday.

items.title: Ex-official describes pressure from UK PM's office, deepening Mandelson row
items.summary: LONDON: A former top foreign ministry official said on Tuesday he had faced "constant pressure" from Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office to speed up the process to install his pick as US ambassador, deepening a row that threatens the British leader. He was quick to turn the focus on Starmer's office. "I think throughout January (2025), honestly, my office, the foreign secretary's office, were under constant pressure," Robbins said.

items.title: RTD, Media Prima to host mega carnival with free helmets, discounts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Road Transport Department (RTD) is partnering with Media Prima Bhd for the JPJ Madani Mega Carnival and Karnival Jom Heboh in conjunction with its 80th anniversary. RTD director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said highlights include a free helmet exchange programme for those who bring their old headgear. Aedy Fadly added that the department is continuously boosting advocacy and enforcement activities to reduce the national road accident rate.

items.title: Rolls-Royce Cullinan, Porsche Taycan among 18 luxury cars seized as KL cops smash drug ring
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — A Rolls-Royce Cullinan, Porsche Taycan 4S and Mercedes-Benz AMG were among 18 luxury vehicles seized by police in a major Klang Valley drug bust. MalaysiaGazette reported today that nineteen suspects were arrested in the raids under Op Empis, with total seizures — including drugs, cash and high-value assets — estimated at RM14.15 million. NCID director CP Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said officers raided multiple locations, including apartments in Taman Wahyu and Kepong, uncovering drugs worth an estimated RM3.1 million.

items.title: Mass shooting reignites push to loosen gun laws in war-torn Ukraine
items.summary: Hours after a gunman took hostages in a Kyiv supermarket on Saturday, shooting seven people dead, many Ukrainians turned to social media to demand the right to bear handguns for their own protection. Many others condemned the idea, in alarm. Civilians in Ukraine are not allowed to carry firearms, and there is no comprehensive law regulating armed self-defence.

items.title: Perak Mufti Dept warns on 'office wife, office husband' trend in workplace relationships
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Perak Mufti Department has issued a warning against the growing "office wife" and "office husband" phenomenon, saying overly close relationships between colleagues of the opposite sex may lead to emotional impropriety and affect workplace harmony and family institutions. The statement said that the terms "office wife" and "office husband" generally referred to close relationships between male and female colleagues who may share personal matters, emotional concerns and daily routines in a manner resembling that of spouses. The department said that maintaining proper boundaries at the workplace was intended to preserve dignity, protect family institutions and uphold harmony within society.

items.title: Mara targets June law change for stronger governance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A draft amendment to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act 1996 is being targeted for tabling in Parliament as early as June, in a move aimed at ensuring the agency's legal framework remains relevant to modern governance needs. Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the amendment is also intended to align the organisation's management with current developments and future challenges, particularly in the areas of integrity and transparency. He said the existing law did not adequately address the separation of powers within the organisation or broader institutional governance.

items.title: [UPDATED] Drug ring using money changers as front busted, 19 arrested
items.summary: He said authorities also seized cash in multiple currencies amounting to an estimated RM4.9 million, believed to be proceeds from drug trafficking. Various types of drugs were seized, including 0.89kg of ecstasy pills, 0.69kg of ketamine, 0.46kg of Erimin 5 and 6.5kg of MDMA powder, as well as equipment used to process drugs," he told a press conference at the Kuala Lumpur police contingent headquarters today. Hussein said follow-up raids in Kuala Lumpur and Kepong led to further arrests.

items.title: Welfare Dept seeks fit next of kin for boy allegedly abused by aunt, uncle
items.summary: KUALA KRAI: A 10-year-old boy who was allegedly abused by his aunt and uncle is in stable condition but remains traumatised. Kelantan Welfare Department director Che Samsuzuki Che Noh said the child is currently under close monitoring by doctors at Kuala Krai Hospital. Che Samsuzuki added that counsellors from the department would conduct activities to help the child cope with the trauma.

items.title: DID records one river in KL exceeding danger level
items.summary: The water level at the station reached 33.03m at 2.30pm today, exceeding the 32.66m danger threshold by 0.37m with an upward trend. The normal threshold for the station is 27.5m, followed by the alert level at 31.66m and the warning level at 32.16m. At Sungai Bidor, the water level was recorded at 3.49m, which is 0.27m above the warning level.

items.title: Police: Kulim officer alleged to have stepped on man’s head to be investigated, focus on arrest SOP compliance
items.summary: KULIM, April 21 — Investigations into an incident involving a man being stepped on by a police officer during an arrest in Kulim, Kedah, are focused on compliance with arrest standard operating procedures (SOPs). Kulim deputy police chief DSP Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng said the Integrity and Standards Compliance Department (JIPS) has been instructed to conduct an investigation into the officer involved. Upon inspecting the suspect’s motorcycle, police found two transparent plastic packets believed to contain ketum juice.

items.title: Modern education needs the wisdom of civilisational history
items.summary: Iran's civilisational experience demonstrates one enduring strength: a deep intellectual tradition grounded in rigor, system, and scientific inquiry. Iran reminds us that knowledge must be deep, structured, and intellectually rigorous. The Nusantara reminds us that knowledge must be ethical, lived, and socially meaningful.

items.title: Ministry ramps up enforcement to curb inflation driven by global disruptions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is stepping up measures to control the supply and prices of goods to shield the local market from inflationary pressures arising from the global supply crisis. She said rising international logistics costs are contributing to higher import prices, affecting the entire domestic supply chain. When logistics costs increase, surely the prices of goods brought in will rise, and there will be costs throughout the supply chain," she said.

items.title: Flash floods in Cameron Highlands draw federal probe, geologists deployed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — The Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry will dispatch geologists to Cameron Highlands following flash floods that sent muddy torrents onto a main road, as authorities move to pinpoint the cause and prevent a repeat, the New Straits Times reported. Heavy rain lasting more than two hours yesterday triggered the flash floods in the Habu Heights area, with runoff from surrounding hills surging onto the Ringlet main road. “The runoff from hilly areas surged onto the main road, causing flash floods.

items.title: Geopolitical tensions open window for Penang to court global business events
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang is seeing a surge in opportunities to host international conferences as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East force organisers to relocate events. Penang Convention and Exhibition Bureau (PCEB) chief executive officer Ashwin Gunasekeran said the shifting landscape has led to a redistribution of major business events, with Asia-Pacific emerging as a key alternative region. Ashwin said Penang has identified 26 international conferences as immediate targets for this year and next.

items.title: Illicit cigarettes make up 60pct of Malaysia market, report says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Illicit cigarettes make up about 60 per cent of Malaysia's market, with six in 10 packs untaxed as syndicates tighten their grip. According to Nielsen, cigarettes are smuggled through unguarded entry points from Vietnam, China and Indonesia, using containers up to 10m long that can carry up to 10 million sticks. If sold in Malaysia, syndicates can make profits of up to RM2 million per container.

items.title: Indonesian expert warns of cross-border security threats in region
items.summary: Dino Hindarto, president of the Indonesian chapter of the Asia Pacific Security Association (APSA), said such threats often spill over beyond national borders. Hindarto also said regular dialogue and stronger regional coordination are needed to enhance border security and tackle transnational crime. Drawing on past regional experience, including terrorism incidents, Hindarto said cooperation through intelligence-sharing, joint training and coordinated exercises has proven effective in mitigating threats.

items.title: Kundasang earthquake causes no damage or injuries: Fire Department
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A weak earthquake struck Kundasang, Ranau at 3.02am today, but no structural damage or injuries have been reported. The Sabah Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said in a statement that a monitoring operation, which began at 6.35am, involved inspections of shop premises, government buildings and public areas around Ranau town. No injuries or subsequent incidents have been reported," the statement said.

items.title: Geologists to probe Cameron Highlands flash floods
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: The Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry will deploy geologists to Cameron Highlands to study the recent flash floods and provide technical assistance. Its minister, Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, said the ministry would work with several agencies, including the Public Works Department (JKR), the Department of Irrigation and Drainage (JPS) and local authorities, to identify the cause of the incident and measures to prevent a recurrence. Heavy rain lasting more than two hours on Sunday triggered flash floods in the Habu Heights area in Cameron Highlands.

items.title: Father of two jailed for shoplifting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A father of two was jailed for one month by the magistrate's court today after stealing items from a supermarket including deodorant, insect repellent, canned sardines, mayonnaise and flour. Magistrate Faezahnoor Hassan also imposed a RM300 fine on Mohd Rashid Mohd Noh, 44, an e-hailing driver, after he pleaded guilty. The magistrate ordered him to begin serving his sentence immediately.

items.title: Malaysia faces shortage of over 5,900 veterinarians
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: Malaysia is facing a shortage of more than 5,900 veterinary professionals, with only about 2,600 active practitioners currently serving the country, says Veterinary Services Department director-general Dr Mohd Noor Hisham Mohd Haron. He said the gap had become increasingly pressing amid rising demand for veterinary services, driven by higher pet ownership, the expansion of the livestock industry, and stricter export certification and international compliance requirements. "Compared with international benchmarks, the country actually needs at least 8,500 veterinarians to meet current demand.

items.title: Airod, Airbus partner to strengthen maintenance for RMAF's A400M fleet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aircraft maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) provider Airod Sdn Bhd has signed a memorandum of understanding with Airbus Defence and Space to strengthen maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) capabilities for the Royal Malaysian Air Force's (RMAF) Airbus A400M fleet. National Aerospace and Defence Industries Sdn Bhd group president Datuk Edron Hayata Ahmad said the partnership marked a key step in strengthening Malaysia's defence aerospace ecosystem. "This partnership with Airbus Defence and Space marks an important step in strengthening Malaysia's defence aerospace ecosystem.

items.title: Terengganu divorce cases hit seven-year high, assemblyman warns of social crisis
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A sharp rise in divorce cases in Terengganu is an early warning of a social crisis that requires urgent and comprehensive action, said Ladang assemblyman Zuraida Mohd Noor. She said the state recorded 3,810 divorce cases in 2025, a significant increase from 2,798 in 2024 and 1,802 in 2023, marking the highest figures in seven years. Data from the Syariah Judiciary Department also placed Terengganu among the eight states with the highest number of cases in the first nine months of last year.

items.title: All poultry farms in Perak to adopt closed-house system by 2027
items.summary: State Rural Development, Plantation, Agriculture and Food Industry Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Zolkafly Harun said the move is aimed at accelerating the industry's shift towards a more modern, sustainable and environmentally friendly model. Zolkafly said the transition would also help address long-standing environmental concerns, including fly infestations and foul odours commonly linked to open-house poultry farms. He said 177 farms are still operating using open-house systems, including 52 owned by licensed and active Bumiputera entrepreneurs statewide.

items.title: [UPDATED] Drug-laced vape liquids containing furanyl fentanyl seized in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police recently uncovered the sale of drug-laced liquids containing furanyl fentanyl following raids during a joint operation in Sentul and Cheras. Federal Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said police seized nine bottles of the liquid drug weighing 16.86kg during a raid held under Op Vape 1.0 on April 13. Hussein said investigations showed the liquid was used to lace vape cartridges with the highly dangerous drug.

items.title: Iran hangs man convicted of setting mosque on fire during pre-war protests
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran executed on Tuesday a man convicted of helping set fire to a major mosque in Tehran and of collaborating with Israel and the United States during pre-war protests, the judiciary said. Iran has in recent weeks carried out multiple executions of people linked to the protests, whom authorities accused of acting on behalf of Israel, the United States or opposition groups, including members of the banned People's Mujahedin (MEK). Iran has been at war with the United States and Israel since Feb 28.

items.title: Labuan Customs seizes three containers filled with RM343,400 worth of liquor
items.summary: LABUAN: The Labuan Customs Department seized three 20-foot containers of liquor worth RM343,400 after a company allegedly exceeded its import quota. Labuan Customs director Aspalila Ag Tuah said the enforcement operations team acted on intelligence to intercept the shipment. Aspalila said liquor importation is strictly regulated, requiring adherence to quotas and the use of authorised tax stamps under Regulation 42(1) of the Customs Regulations 2019.

items.title: Rising waters push KL river past danger mark, says Irrigation and Drainage Dept
items.summary: The water level at the station stood at 33.03m, or 0.37m above the danger threshold, with an upward trend recorded at 2.30pm. Elsewhere, water levels at two stations — Sungai Bidor near Changkat Jong in Hilir Perak and Sungai Perlis at Bukit Tok Poh (F2) in Kangar, Perlis — were recorded above warning levels. At Sungai Perlis, the water level reached 2.06m, or 0.06m above the warning threshold, with an upward trend observed at about 2.30pm.

items.title: Malaysians should be prudent in fuel to stretch energy buffer beyond June
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians are urged to be prudent in fuel consumption to stretch the country's energy buffer beyond June as global uncertainties are expected to persist, said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong. He said the country remains in a relatively comfortable position for now, but early conservation is necessary to ensure supply stability in the months ahead. Liew said the evolving global environment requires Malaysia not only to manage near-term resources but also to strengthen its economic fundamentals, particularly in addressing vulnerabilities within supply chains.

items.title: Belgium slams Israel's Lebanon actions as 'totally unacceptable'
items.summary: LUXEMBOURG: Israel's actions in Lebanon are "totally unacceptable", Belgian Foreign Minister Maxime Prevot said on Tuesday ahead of a meeting with EU foreign ministers in Luxembourg. "Israel's conduct is completely unacceptable. He also said Belgium is calling for at least a partial suspension of the EU's Association's Agreement with Israel, adding that Belgium is "aware that a full suspension is probably out of reach given the positions of the various European countries."

items.title: Drink-driving: Woman jailed, fined RM13,000 over Genting Klang crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 42-year-old woman was jailed for one day and fined RM13,000 after pleading guilty to drink-driving following a crash involving a motorcycle at Jalan Genting Klang on Monday. The accident occurred at 8.22pm at a traffic light junction near the Road Transport Department (JPJ) in Wangsa Maju, involving a white BMW X1 sDrive20i and a red Yamaha LC135 motorcycle. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said a report was lodged at 10.15pm the same night, while a video of the incident surfaced on social media the following morning.

items.title: Israel vows full force to disarm Hizbollah despite ceasefire
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said Tuesday that his country's campaign in Lebanon relied on both military and diplomatic pressure to disarm Iran-allied Hizbollah. Though a truce between Israel and Lebanon took effect Friday, Israeli troops are still present and actively fighting Hizbollah in Lebanon's south, with Katz saying Sunday that troops would use "full force" if threatened. Israel and Lebanon are still engaged in talks under US mediation to prolong the current truce.

items.title: PM: Govt to announce support measures for those out of work, including unemployed graduates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 (Bernama) -- The government will soon announce additional measures to support those who were out of work due to the global supply crisis. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiatives would include unemployed graduates and diploma holders, so as to ensure their skills are not wasted. Also present were Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour and Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican.

items.title: Father of two jailed, fined RM300 over Cheras supermarket theft involving sardines and other daily essentials
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — A father of two was sentenced to one month in jail and fined RM300 by the Magistrates’ Court today after pleading guilty to stealing various daily necessities, including two tins of sardines. According to Utusan Malaysia , Magistrate Faezahnoor Hassan handed down the sentence after Mohd Rashid Mohd Noh, 44, admitted to the charge when it was read to him. According to the charge, Mohd Rashid stole 20 items — including two tins of sardines, all-purpose flour, dishwashing liquid, bath soap and an air freshener — worth RM404.22 from an Aeon supermarket in Taman Maluri, Cheras, at 8.10pm on April 18.

items.title: Matrade turns 47 offices abroad into ‘frontline hubs’ to shield exporters from trade turmoil, says Reezal Merican
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican today reassured that Malaysia’s exporters are being supported through a comprehensive mitigation strategy as global trade disruptions intensify. The Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) chairman said the agency is stepping up efforts to diversify into new markets and strengthen small and medium enterprises (SMEs). “We are also focusing on non-conflict markets to enhance access and expand into new opportunities,” Reezal Merican told a press briefing at the Matrade headquarters here today.

items.title: Selangor flood crisis not a quick fix, four-year timeline needed, says exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Flooding that continues to plague residents in Selangor is unlikely to be resolved for another four years, as the state’s flood mitigation projects remain ongoing and incomplete. Online , State Infrastructure and Agriculture Exco Datuk Izham Hashim said river widening and deepening works are still underway and will take time before delivering a broader impact. Izham said Selangor has adopted modern water management technologies from the Netherlands, China and Germany, but their effectiveness is constrained by the state’s high rainfall and rapid development.

items.title: Sarawak Water: Viral message of Sungai Liku reservoir drying up, water rationing is fake
items.summary: MIRI, April 21 — Sarawak Water Sdn Bhd has dismissed a viral message circulating on social media and WhatsApp claiming the Sungai Liku reservoir is drying up due to hot weather and urging consumers to store water in anticipation of possible rationing. “The water treatment plant continues to operate at full capacity to ensure an uninterrupted supply of treated water to all consumers,” it said. Sarawak Water also called on the public to stop circulating the false message, adding that any official notices on water supply disruptions would be issued via its official website, verified Facebook page, or through recognised media organisations in Sarawak.

items.title: Kota Tinggi Bypass Bridge nears finish line, set to open July 28, says deputy works minister
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, April 21 — The Kota Tinggi Bypass Bridge project, built at a cost of more than RM104 million, is now 85 per cent complete and scheduled to be operational on July 28, said Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan. He said the steel arch bridge will feature four lanes in two directions, spanning 464 metres in length and 22.6 metres in width. Meanwhile, Ahmad said the Kota Tinggi JKR is also carrying out maintenance works on 772.91 kilometres (km) of federal roads and 135.59km of state roads.

items.title: DPM: Subsidy policies will be data-driven, no sudden shock changes expected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — The government will not introduce abrupt or sudden changes to fuel subsidy policies despite mounting pressure from geopolitical tensions in West Asia, according to Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. Fadillah said the government recognises that the conflict in West Asia could persist for one to two years, but any action taken will prioritise protecting the majority of Malaysians. Fadillah, who is also Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said the government will maintain fuel subsidies despite a sharp rise in costs driven by the global energy crisis.

items.title: No ‘wait-and-see’: Perak braces for West Asia spillover, sets up twin committees to shield state economy
items.summary: IPOH, April 21 — The Perak government will establish two special committees to implement a crisis impact mitigation plan amid the ongoing West Asia tensions, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said. He said the proposed committees — the Special Committee on Crisis Impact Mitigation and the Crisis Impact Monitoring Committee — were recommended following a state-level coordination meeting chaired by the state secretary. “The specific roles of these two committees will be detailed at the upcoming State Executive Council meeting, followed by the phased implementation of mitigation and intervention measures,” he said.

items.title: Home Ministry to probe viral incident of cop allegedly stepping on suspect’s head in Kulim
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 21 — The police have been directed to conduct a comprehensive investigation into a viral incident involving a policeman stepping on a man’s head during an arrest in Kulim, Kedah, recently. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said he had contacted Kedah Police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah to expedite the investigation, which will also look into whether the personnel adhered to standard operating procedures (SOP). “I have contacted the police chief to expedite the investigation, including a check on whether enforcement procedures were complied with.

items.title: Home minister: Ministry will assist victims of Sandakan fire in replacing documents
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 21 — The Home Ministry (KDN), through the National Registration Department, will assist victims of Sunday’s fire tragedy in Sandakan to replace their identity documents in accordance with established procedures. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the process of replacing the documents will be subject to proper verification and will not be automatically granted to all victims indiscriminately. Saifuddin Nasution said that under existing standard operating procedures (SOP), replacement identity documents will be issued to citizens affected by incidents such as fires and similar disasters.

items.title: Deadline extended: Selangor grants final August exit for Jalan Sungai Buloh nurseries
items.summary: According to national daily Sinar Harian , State Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the extension was granted after appeals from operators, despite the eviction process having been in motion since 2019 with multiple notices issued and policy decisions made. “The state government has gone through all processes, including the State Executive Council (MMKN), since 2021. Izham said 38 operators across 66 lots are affected, with some holding more than one lot.

items.title: DBKL cracks down on ‘ulat foto’ at KLCC, Saloma Link with 23 compounds, 28 seizures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has issued 23 compounds and carried out 28 seizures of equipment belonging to photo touts, commonly known as “ ulat foto ”, under Op Lens as of yesterday. “Meanwhile, the Immigration Department (JIM) and the National Registration Department (JPN) inspected 31 individuals, including checks on identification documents. “Four individuals were issued notices of document retention by JPN under the National Registration Act 1959 in accordance with Regulation 21(1)(c) of the National Registration Regulations 1990 (Amendment 2007).

items.title: Sabah MACC arrests civil servant over alleged RM1.3m false claims
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 21 — The Sabah Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) arrested a civil servant suspected of submitting falsified documents linked to cleaning work claims worth approximately RM1.3 million. According to a source, the male suspect, in his 40s, was detained at around 11am on Monday while giving his statement at the MACC office in Kota Kinabalu. Meanwhile, Sabah MACC director Datuk Mohd Fuad Bee Basrah, when contacted, confirmed the arrest, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: PM Anwar announces further RM5b addition to SJPP financing scheme, RM10b in total, to benefit MSMEs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has announced an additional RM5 billion facility under the Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan (SJPP) scheme to assist micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) facing the impact of the West Asia crisis. This additional facility brings the total facility to RM10 billion. Anwar said the governor of Bank Negara Malaysia will provide details of the additional facility in the near future.

items.title: Perlis MB banks on Perikatan unity after no-confidence talk dismissed
items.summary: Abu Bakar, who is also the Kuala Perlis assemblyman from Bersatu, said the show of support would help reinforce the state government. “I am grateful for the efforts undertaken by PN and the Perlis PAS committee, which met yesterday and decided to uphold the PN state government because we must respect one another. On the postponed state assembly sitting, Abu Bakar said a new date would be announced soon.

items.title: Old Klang Road wrong-way driver gets three months’ jail as KL High Court says three days was too light
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — The High Court here has increased the jail term of a former e-hailing driver to three months for reckless and dangerous driving, ruling that a tougher sentence was warranted despite a plea for leniency based on his family circumstances. On April 6, the magistrates’ court sentenced Wong to three days’ jail and fined him RM6,000 after he pleaded guilty. The court also suspended his driving licence for five years.

items.title: Court orders additional 10 months’ jail for 33 men who stormed Taman Shamelin KTV after previously imposing fines
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — A High Court decision has extended the sentences of 33 men involved in an attack on a KTV outlet in Taman Shamelin, ordering them to serve an additional 10 months in prison. According to The Star, Justice Datuk Aslam Zainuddin delivered the ruling today while reviewing a prosecution appeal against a Magistrate's Court judgment from earlier in the month. The lower court had previously imposed fines ranging from RM2,000 to RM3,000 on the men.

items.title: Geopolitical tensions slash Malaysia’s West Asia trade by 30.4pc, says Matrade chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Malaysia’s trade with West Asia fell 30.4 per cent to RM21.41 billion amid ongoing geopolitical tensions, said Datuk Seri Reezal Merican Naina Merican. The chairman of the Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) said exports to the region dropped 23.6 per cent to RM9.87 billion, mainly due to lower shipments of palm oil, petroleum products and electrical and electronic (E&E) goods. “Since West Asia accounts for only about 2.7 per cent (RM21.41 billion) of Malaysia’s total trade, disruptions there have limited direct impact.

items.title: Flooded stretches on Jalan Ampang Hilir, Jalan Kuching snarl KL traffic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Several major roads in the city centre were hit by flash floods today after continuous rain since morning, disrupting traffic flow. In a Facebook post, the Kuala Lumpur Control Centre (KLCCC) under Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) said the persistent rainfall had caused water to accumulate at multiple locations. Vehicles drive through floodwaters along Jalan Kuching in Kuala Lumpur after heavy rain.

items.title: Matrade chairman: Malaysia’s trade shrugs off global jitters, posts 9.3pc growth in March
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Malaysia’s trade grew 9.3 per cent year-on-year to RM272.95 billion in March 2026, driven by strong demand for electrical and electronic (E&E) goods. “For the first quarter, exports expanded 12.7 per cent to RM426.53 billion, pushing the trade surplus up 54 per cent to RM63.22 billion. Among the non-traditional markets that recorded growth were Sudan (RM90.8 million, up 322.5 per cent), the Democratic Republic of the Congo (RM86.3 million, up 165.3 per cent), Uganda (RM40.4 million, up 284.5 per cent), Zimbabwe (RM21.2 million, up 207.7 per cent), Haiti (RM73.1 million, up 940.6 per cent), Paraguay (RM12.6 million, up 161.7 per cent), Guyana (RM20.9 million, up 70.3 per cent), Uzbekistan (RM101.5 million, up 14.7 per cent), Ethiopia (RM125.3 million, up 91.6 per cent), Tanzania (RM461.1 million, up 38.4 per cent), Bangladesh (RM3.44 billion, up 33.7 per cent), Egypt (RM777.7 million, up 18.6 per cent), and Libya (RM29.5 million, up 31.6 per cent).

items.title: Cemetery tryst couple freed after Penang High Court revises jail term to time served following accepted representation
items.summary: In allowing the criminal revision, judge Rofiah Mohamad maintained the conviction against M. Jegathesan, 58, and Halila Abu Bakar, 37, but replaced the original sentence with imprisonment from the date of arrest on March 23 until the disposal of the revision today. “However, the 12-month sentence is replaced with a term of imprisonment from the date of arrest until today. She said the agreement maintained the conviction but sought a reduction in the custodial term.

items.title: Syed Saddiq warns new prosecutor role could be among Malaysia’s most powerful, calls for checks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — The proposed law to separate the role of public prosecutor (PP) from the attorney general (AG) could result in the former being one of the most powerful persons in Malaysia, according to Muar MP Syed Saddiq Abdul Rahman. For this reason alone is why he said the power to appoint the PP should not be decided by one person, but decentralised during the selection and appointment process. But Syed Saddiq said that the AG currently comes under the Prime Minister’s Office.

items.title: Perak struggles to attract major hotels as tourists skip overnight stays
items.summary: IPOH, April 21 — Difficulties in attracting major international hotel brands to Perak were highlighted in the State Legislative Assembly, as most tourists visiting the state prefer day trips rather than overnight stays. Perak Tourism, Industry, Investment and Corridor Development Committee chairman Loh Sze Yee said the state continues to be seen largely as a day-trip destination among travellers. Likewise, visitors from Penang often come to Perak for the day but choose to stay overnight in Kuala Lumpur instead,” he said.

items.title: Food vs fuel: Survey shows what Malaysians and Thais fear most as oil jitters rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — A new survey has found food and fuel prices topping consumer worries in Malaysia and Thailand as tensions in the Strait of Hormuz keep oil markets volatile. Food vs fuel divide In Malaysia, 35.8 per cent of respondents cited food and groceries as their top concern, compared to 24.1 per cent who pointed to fuel despite existing subsidies. In Thailand, 34.4 per cent were most worried about fuel and transport costs, while 26.8 per cent cited food prices.

items.title: Act now, not after harm: Perak Sultan urges early AI regulation at global forum
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 21 — Artificial intelligence (AI), genetic engineering and other modern technologies should be regulated before they are deployed, the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, said today. Sultan Nazrin cautioned that the cost of governing in retrospect could prove irreversible, particularly in the climate and ecological domains. Sultan Nazrin also called on Asean member states to invest urgently in AI literacy to help communities combat deepfakes, synthetic media and algorithmically amplified disinformation campaigns.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan Undang dispute MB’s account of council advice
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 21 — Four Undang in Negeri Sembilan have challenged Mentri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun’s account of a key council meeting, disputing his claim that the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) had advised the removal of the Undang of Sungei Ujong, Datuk Mubarak Dohak. In a joint statement issued today, as reported by The Star , Undang Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad, Undang Johol Datuk Muhammed Abdullah, Undang Rembau Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin, and Mubarak said a media statement released by the mentri besar’s office on April 17 was inaccurate. The four further expressed disagreement with another statement issued by Aminuddin on April 20, and said they supported a declaration made on April 19 regarding changes to the state’s leadership.
